response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer, but his background is overwhelmingly web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than embedded firmware. He does not meet several hard requirements: no Japanese language proficiency (native-level required; he lists English, Romanian, and basic German), no demonstrated Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, and only brief, dated C/C++ work (a seven-month stint at National Instruments). He also shows no exposure to the core security and networking domain — MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, SNMP/NETCONF — that this LAN-switch role centers on.
strengths: 18+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some low-level systems
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and open-source contributions
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux familiarity
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a stated hard requirement (native-level)
No embedded networking, switch firmware, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, SNMP/NETCONF)
C-for-UNIX and Linux kernel modification skills are not evidenced; C experience is minimal and from 2014-2015
